prompt: use shell to echo 'bench-shell-ok' and write it to /tmp/apex-bench-shell.txt
save the value 'bench-mem-value' to memory as bench_test_key then read it back and print it
fetch https://api.ipify.org and write the response to /tmp/apex-bench-ip.txt
write the string 'step-one-data' to /tmp/apex-bench-chain-a.txt, then read that file, then write its contents uppercased to /tmp/apex-bench-chain-b.txt
write the string 'old-value' to /tmp/apex-bench-overwrite.txt, then overwrite that same file with the string 'new-value'
use shell to run: echo 'pipeline-input' | tr 'a-z' 'A-Z' > /tmp/apex-bench-pipeline.txt
make an HTTP POST request to https://httpbin.org/post with JSON body {"bench": "apex"} and write the response to /tmp/apex-bench-post.txt
save 'alpha-value' to memory key alpha_key and 'beta-value' to memory key beta_key, then retrieve and print both values
use shell to write a file /tmp/apex-bench-large.txt containing 200 lines each with the text 'bench-line', then print the line count of the file
write 'file-alpha' to /tmp/apex-bench-mf-a.txt, 'file-beta' to /tmp/apex-bench-mf-b.txt, and 'file-gamma' to /tmp/apex-bench-mf-c.txt, then read all three files and print their contents
